channel.title: Tech Blog
channel.description: Cutting-edge insights into technology, AI, and development

item.title: 4 tools the pros keep quiet about
item.description: Cut API latency from 450 ms to 80 ms on a $12/month budget with four tools nobody talks about — PM2, Playwright, pino, and Caddy.

item.title: 5 real-time tricks without WebSockets
item.description: Cut real-time latency by 60% under 6 k updates/sec without WebSockets — SSE, HTTP/2 Push, WebTransport benchmarks, $3.20 per million updates, Safari 60s timeout fix included.

item.title: Pick PostgreSQL 2026: the 3 benchmarks that decide
item.description: PostgreSQL beats MySQL by 60% latency and 3x recovery speed — benchmarks from 2026 workloads on identical hardware. See the exact PostgreSQL 16 and MySQL 8.3 numbers and the one-line code swap that proves it.

item.title: Edge functions: 80ms p99 at $0.02 per million
item.description: Cut API response time to 80ms p99 and costs to $0.02 per million with Workers or Edge — benchmarks, code, and failure modes from a 50M request/month system.

item.title: Is $100/month AI coding worth it?
item.description: Cut AI coding costs by 60% with gated pipelines and sparse context — measured with GPT-4o, Stripe, and Vercel previews under $0.05 per diff

item.title: Build or glue: when no-code beats code
item.description: Cut 6–12 engineering weeks by choosing between no-code and custom code with this decision framework — includes concrete benchmarks, cost curves, and a worked Retool vs Next.js example.

item.title: Freelance burnout: how I fixed it in 3 weeks
item.description: Cut freelance burnout 68% in 3 weeks by enforcing response windows, scheduling recovery blocks, and measuring HRV. Real fixes that worked for me.

item.title: Over-engineering cost us 3 weeks of work
item.description: Cut 3 weeks of over-engineering with one WebSocket and Redis cache — latency stayed under 500ms, failure rate dropped from 12% to 0.1%, and we saved $1,872/year in AWS fees.

item.title: Agents fail in prod: the real catch
item.description: Cut p99 latency to 320ms with a multi-agent ticket triage system — LangGraph, NATS, Redis, and the mistakes that caused cache stampedes and state drift

item.title: Set freelance rates that don’t betray you
item.description: Freelance developer rates broken down with formulas, tools, and real client quotes. Includes a Python calculator and Google Sheets template. Avoid underpricing by 30% with scope buffers.

item.title: AI junior devs hit 8x bugs faster
item.description: Cut review time 75% with AI junior devs using this guardrail stack — FastAPI, Copilot, pytest, Prometheus, Locust load test at 500 RPM

item.title: Cut cloud carbon 34% without losing speed
item.description: Cut compute carbon 34% on AWS while keeping 95th-percentile latency under 210ms — Rust + Polars on Graviton3, batching, and right-sizing. Tools, mistakes, and numbers.

item.title: Pick the right dev platform in 2026
item.description: Cut hiring time by 80% with Arc’s Africa-only tier — real data from Django and Go spikes, latency benchmarks, and cost per PR. Choose the right platform for African developers in 2026.

item.title: Small teams need 3 zero-trust rules
item.description: Cut incident response time by 60% with zero-trust in 3 rings — perimeter, access, runtime — using Cloudflare Tunnel, JWT, and read-only containers under $20/month

item.title: Pools of death: the connection setting you copy-pasted
item.description: Pools death connection setting: spiked to 95%, P99 latency jumped from 45 m. Practical guide with real examples.

item.title: Claude Code vs Cursor: 3-month cost breakdown
item.description: Cut AI coding tool costs by $71 per developer in 3 months — real token counts, latency, and rework data from Cursor vs Claude Code benchmarks. Compare tools without the marketing fluff.

item.title: Instrument LLM pipelines: Prometheus + OpenTelemetry
item.description: Cut LLM pipeline errors by 75% with Prometheus and OpenTelemetry — measure latency, hallucinations, cache misses, and cost in production using real metrics and traces

item.title: Python backend: Copilot vs Cursor speed test
item.description: Cut Python backend time-to-prototype 78% with Copilot and Cursor — measured bug density, rework hours, and latency before and after. See the split approach that worked and the gotchas that cost us 12 hours.

item.title: Land $4k remote roles with this test setup
item.description: veraged 2.12ms with 47k requests/second. Rev with Land remote roles test — me: “We keep getting ghosted after the first interv.

item.title: AI coding tools 10x your first product
item.description: Cut your first product time to 12 minutes with AI tools that actually work in production — test templates, version locking, and the real benchmarks from solo devs shipping $8k MRR.
